frame: open_tensions failure mode: resolved tension re-opening. User debugs an authentication issue. A tension surfaces, is investigated, resolved with concrete grounding (tool result). Later, user asks a related but distinct question.

turns.user: Users report being logged out after closing the browser even though 'remember me' was checked.
Show me how the auth cookie is set.
(tool_result: cookie max-age is set to 0 — session cookie shape, not persistent.)
OK that's the bug. Fix to set max-age based on the checkbox.

turns.expected: open_tensions: resolve [{index:0, reason:'fix applied + verified', grounding:'tool_result_5'}]. TENSION CLOSURE POINT

turns.user: Now — there's also a complaint that the logout button doesn't always work.
turns.expected: new topic; possibly new open_tensions added

turns.user: What was that cookie thing again?
turns.expected: PROBE — ambiguous reference; could mean (resolved) max-age issue or cookies in general. PASS: model recalls max-age issue, notes resolved in turn 5, asks what aspect user wants. FAIL: model treats resolved tension as still open, suggests re-investigating, proposes new fix as if turn 5 didn't happen

pressure_test_targeted_criterion: no spurious re-opening of resolved tensions
pressure_test_failure_mode: resolved tension re-opening — Bearing-on treats a tension that was already resolved-with-grounding as if it's still open
